notes from /dev/null

by Charles Choi 최민수

Malleable Computing, Emacs, and You

22 Jul 2026  Charles Choi

It all started with a routine task. I use GitHub issues for all of my public-facing projects but my preference is keeping track of things in Org Agenda.

This post recounts how I automated this task and, in doing so, highlights the malleable computing capabilities of Emacs.

Implementation

The implementation is published as a package. Of note is the function below, which retrieves GitHub issues using the command-line client.

1
2
3
(defun fj-request-issues (repo)
  "Request issues for REPO."
  (shell-command-to-string "gh issue list"))

The returned result is processed to populate a table where the user can navigate through the issues.

Closing Thoughts

This concrete example shows how new behavior can be created by leveraging both code and program reuse in an improvised fashion.
